name: creating-pull-requests
description: Creates pull requests with generated descriptions. Triggered when: PR creation, pull request, merge request, code review preparation.
capabilities: Checks current branch and git status before creating PR
Reviews commit history against main branch
Pushes local branches to remote with tracking
Creates PRs with structured markdown descriptions
Adds labels, assignees, and target branches to PRs
Generates completion reports with PR URL and details
author: 1gy
securitySummary: This skill is a pure documentation-based prompt wrapper for git and GitHub CLI operations. The static scanner produced 35 false positives due to misclassification: template placeholders like <branch-name> were flagged as 'weak cryptographic algorithms', and standard shell commands were flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. In reality, this skill only documents standard git commands (branch, status, push) and GitHub CLI PR creation. No code execution occurs beyond user-initiated git/gh commands. The original AI audit correctly classified this as safe.
valueStatement: Creating pull requests manually takes time and often results in incomplete descriptions. This skill automates PR creation with structured templates, ensuring consistent documentation and faster code reviews.
userTitle: Create GitHub pull requests with templates
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated
Only works with GitHub repositories, not other Git hosting services
Does not handle merge conflicts or failed checks
Cannot create PRs without appropriate repository permissions

useCases.title: Streamline PR workflows
useCases.description: Quickly create well-documented pull requests with consistent templates and test checklists

useCases.title: Submit quality contributions
useCases.description: Generate professional PR descriptions that help maintainers review and merge contributions faster

useCases.title: Improve review documentation
useCases.description: Ensure every PR includes clear summaries and test plans for faster, more effective reviews

promptTemplates.title: Create a simple PR
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to create a basic pull request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pull request with the title '[Brief description]' describing my changes

promptTemplates.title: PR with test checklist
promptTemplates.scenario: When preparing a PR with testing requirements
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pull request titled '[Feature name]' and include test checklist items: [Test item 1], [Test item 2]

promptTemplates.title: PR to non-main branch
promptTemplates.scenario: When targeting a different base branch
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a draft PR for my feature branch targeting the 'develop' branch with summary of changes

promptTemplates.title: Full PR with labels
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need all PR options configured
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pull request with title '[Your title]', base branch 'main', assign to me, add 'enhancement' label, and include this summary: [Your summary]

bestPractices: Run git status and review changes before creating a PR to ensure only intended modifications are included
Use descriptive titles that summarize the change in under 60 characters for better visibility
Fill in all test checklist items to help reviewers understand what verification is needed
antiPatterns: Creating PRs without reviewing the diff first may include unintended changes
Using vague titles like 'fix' or 'update' makes it harder to track changes in project history
Skipping the test plan makes it difficult for reviewers to verify the implementation works correctly

faq.question: Does this skill work with GitLab or Bitbucket?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses GitHub CLI (gh) and only works with GitHub repositories.

faq.question: What permissions does the GitHub CLI need?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI requires authentication and repo scope permissions to create pull requests.

faq.question: Can I create draft pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --draft flag when creating a PR to mark it as a draft requiring more work.

faq.question: Is my code or data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: Code is only sent to GitHub via the official gh CLI when creating a PR. No third-party services.

faq.question: What if the PR creation fails?
faq.answer: The skill will report any errors from gh CLI such as permission.

faq.question: What if the PR creation fails due to permission issues or branch conflicts?
faq.answer: The skill will report any errors from gh CLI such as permission issues or branch conflicts. You may need to resolve conflicts locally before attempting to create the PR again.

faq.question: How is this different from manual PR creation?
faq.answer: This skill provides templates, automates branch pushing, ensures consistent description format, and reduces setup time.
